snippet.title: Biden’s “Plan” To Fix Inflation Is Insulting To Your Intelligence
snippet.description:
Joe Biden recently published an opinion piece in the Wall Street Journal detailing his plan, to the extent he has one, to deal with the inflation crisis. What the opinion piece reveals, more than anything else, is that Biden not only has no real conception of what is causing prices to rise, but he also is woefully short on ideas for what to do about the problem. 

In this conversation with Jimmy, The New School’s visiting economics professor Richard Wolff tears into Biden and the Democratic Party for a lack of imagination and failing to rise to the moment when so many Americans are struggling.

snippet.title: Dependent Audit - Are the People on your Health Plan Actually Eligible?
snippet.description:
What the heck is a Dependent Audit?  A dependent audit, or a dependent eligibility audit, is something a self-funded employer does to ensure the dependents enrolled on a health plan are actually eligible to have medical coverage.

2:04 - Podcast Start

Keith Bird, VP of Sales for AmWINS came on the Self-Funded with Spencer Podcast to discuss the fiduciary responsibility that Self-Funded Employers have to make sure their employees are not enrolling ineligible dependents, whether intentionally or unintentionally, onto an employer-sponsored health plan.

Unless you have a good way of validating the eligibility of enrollees manually (you probably don't) then you likely have dependents on your plan today that are not supposed to be there.  Keith estimates that about 3% of dependents on average on any given plan are not eligible.

Whether it is a person enrolled as a spouse when they actually aren't married, or an enrolled child on the plan that turns out to be a cousin, the cost of healthcare is so great that many people find the need to defraud their employers for coverage.
tags: am I eligible for health Insurance
is my child eligible for my healht plan

snippet.title: Can Companies Maintain the Pledge of Workplace DEI? | The Atlantic Festival 2022
snippet.description:
After the murder of George Floyd sparked global protests and calls for action, companies pledged to prioritize DEI both internally and in the communities they serve. Two years after the protests ended and the political discourse has refocused, where do those promises stand? Watch Atlantic journalists with DEI experts including ColorComm’s Lauren Wesley Wilson; Autism Community Ventures’ Maureen Dunne; Georgetown University’s Leslie Crutchfield; Wells Fargo’s Stephen DeStefani; and others discuss at The Atlantic Festival.

Grounded in the 160-year legacy of the magazine, AtlanticLIVE brings the unparalleled journalism of The Atlantic to life through singular event experiences, on stage and off. 

For more information, please visit: https://www.theatlantic.com/live/

snippet.title: Uvalde Police WON’T COOPERATE With Police Investigation
snippet.description:
Uvalde police have now announced that they will not be cooperating with investigators looking into the multiple failures that allowed the shooter to massacre 19 students and two teachers at Robb Elementary School in Uvalde. In response to the news, wags on social media have ripped into the police, inquiring why they would stonewall if they have nothing to hide and if they aren’t aware that the best thing to do when dealing with police is to comply.

Jimmy and American comedian Kurt Metzger discuss this latest twist in the horrific unfolding story of the Uvalde school shooting.

snippet.title: Banking While Black at Wells Fargo
snippet.description:
Just another day being black and trying to use banks like everyone else. This video follows the 1st encounter, in which this Branch Manager gave every reason to deny me service. From me not being a customer, to originally never seeing such a check before, from choose the word THRU vs AT... I mean, she threw it ALL at me. Needless to say, I originally chose this location because it was much closer than my actual bank. Plus, my daughter is an employee who told me I could cash a Payable at Wells Fargo... at Wells.

I never saw the check go through the verification process my daughter told me to expect, and after telling her what I endured... she told me the experience was flawed. 

What's your opinion? Hoping to gain some legal referrals. This doesn't sit right with me and I want to explore options of discrimination or not. 

I ultimately went to my own bank further and had ZERO issues.

Meanwhile I'm still confused about Wells Fargo & their non account check cashing of a $50.00 check.

Banking While Black - Wells Fargo Fremont, CA MOWRY Location #banking #wellsfargo #viral #discrimination.
